# ADHD Mirror

> A free, clinically-informed ADHD self-assessment for adults. Based on ASRS v1.1 Part A screening methodology developed by the WHO and Harvard Medical School.

ADHD Mirror is a free tool that helps adults understand whether their experiences align with how ADHD presents clinically. It is not a diagnostic tool — it is a self-reflection and self-advocacy tool designed to help people have more informed conversations with their GP or psychiatrist.

## What makes ADHD Mirror different

- Uses a two-tier scoring system: a weighted core signal score across five symptom clusters, plus the ASRS Part A screen (7 questions most predictive of adult ADHD)
- Includes a dedicated masking section — the coping behaviours that cause many people, particularly women, to score lower than their symptoms warrant
- Applies masking as an upward score modifier when high, to account for suppressed symptom presentation
- Covers emotional dysregulation, executive dysfunction, hyperfocus, and functional impairment — not just inattention and hyperactivity
- Generates a GP-ready summary the user can download and take to a clinical appointment
- Completely private — all scoring happens in the browser, nothing is stored or transmitted

## Who it is for

Adults who suspect they might have ADHD, have never been formally assessed, or feel that previous assessments did not capture the full picture. Particularly useful for:

- Women and non-binary people with inattentive ADHD who have been overlooked by standard checklists
- Adults seeking a late diagnosis
- People who mask effectively and score low on simpler tools despite significant symptoms
- Anyone wanting a GP-ready summary before their first appointment

## Assessment structure

The assessment covers seven sections:
1. Childhood symptoms (before age 12)
2. Focus and attention (past 6 months)
3. Energy and impulse
4. Coping and masking
5. Emotions and relationships
6. Functional impairment
7. Differential screening (anxiety, depression, sleep, burnout)

## Scoring methodology

- Core signal: weighted across inattentive (40%), executive dysfunction (20%), hyperactive-impulsive (20%), emotional intensity (10%), hyperfocus (10%)
- Part A screen: 7 ASRS-mapped questions; 4 or more scoring "Often" or "Always" = positive screen
- Masking modifier: upward adjustment of 8–15 points applied when masking score is moderate or high
- Likelihood output: Low, Moderate, or High — not a diagnosis

## Articles

ADHD Mirror includes a library of clinically-grounded articles:

- [What is ADHD masking?](https://adhdmirror.com/articles/what-is-adhd-masking)
- [ADHD in adults: why so many people are diagnosed late](https://adhdmirror.com/articles/adhd-in-adults-late-diagnosis)
- [ADHD vs anxiety: how to tell the difference](https://adhdmirror.com/articles/adhd-vs-anxiety)
- [What does an ADHD assessment actually involve?](https://adhdmirror.com/articles/what-does-adhd-assessment-involve)
- [ADHD in women: why it looks different and gets missed](https://adhdmirror.com/articles/adhd-in-women)

## Important disclaimer

ADHD Mirror is a self-reflection tool only. It does not provide medical advice, a clinical diagnosis, or any form of professional assessment. Always consult a qualified healthcare professional.

## URLs

- Homepage / assessment: https://adhdmirror.com
- Articles: https://adhdmirror.com/articles
- Privacy policy: https://adhdmirror.com/privacy
